summary: Iter 78c follow-up — backend startup migration restart-safety verified. After removing 'lp_smart' from BOTH $elemMatch and array_filters in services.py:709-718, LP labor edits now survive backend restarts. Confirmed across 3 consecutive backend restarts: lab=9.99 on '38 Series Lap 3/8\" x 8\" x 16'' (seeded by iteration_18) AND a newly-set lab=4.25 on '440 Series Trim 4/4\" x 4\" x 16'' both persisted. Vinyl/Ascend lab-zero enforcement remains intact (46 lines, all lab=0). Backend pytest 29/29 green.
critical_code_review_comments: services.py:702-708 comment block correctly documents Iter 69 + Iter 78c: 'lp_smart REMOVED from this filter — LP contractors book labor on the line. Bounded by tab ∈ {vinyl, ascend} only.' Filter arrays at lines 711 and 716 both contain only ['vinyl','ascend'] — matches the comment.
Idempotent matcher `lab: {$ne: 0}` is preserved on both the $elemMatch and the array_filter so subsequent restarts are no-ops on already-zeroed vinyl/ascend lines.

Recommend (non-blocking) adding a one-shot pytest that PUTs lab=X on an lp_smart line, calls the seed/migration function directly, then asserts the lab survived — would catch any future regression here automatically. The current test_lp_waste_roundtrip.py covers waste but not the lab restart-survival path.

success_rate.frontend: N/A (iteration_18 fully covered UI; not re-tested per main agent guidance)

seed_data_creation: Set lab=4.25 / qty=14 on '440 Series Trim 4/4" x 4" x 16'' line of EST-723351 (id f27a3c6f-00c6-4401-94a0-33f22eb6212e) — in addition to iteration_18's existing 9.99/255 on the 38 Series Lap line. Both LP labor values now sitting on the same estimate for visual verification if Howard opens it.
context_for_next_testing_agent: EST-723351 (id f27a3c6f-00c6-4401-94a0-33f22eb6212e) now has TWO LP lines with non-zero lab as test residue: '38 Series Lap' lab=9.99/qty=255 (from iter18) and '440 Series Trim' lab=4.25/qty=14 (from iter19). Both survive backend restarts. Howard can reset via orange ↺ badge if undesired. The backend startup migration only zeros vinyl + ascend lines now.

rca_of_the_issue: Iter 78c originally relaxed only the frontend lock (SectionAccordion.jsx) while leaving the backend startup migration (services.py:707-717) configured to zero lab on lp_smart at every restart. In-session edits worked, but `ensure_tiers_seeded()` runs on every backend boot and would wipe all saved LP labor. The follow-up fix removes 'lp_smart' from BOTH the $elemMatch tab list (line 711) AND the array_filters (line 716), so only vinyl + ascend are affected. Verified end-to-end via 3 backend restarts — LP labor values 9.99 + 4.25 both survived; vinyl/ascend remain zeroed.
